GitHub Pages "Build Failure" — the .nojekyll Fix for Hexo and Static Sites
Lately deploys of Hexo to GitHub Pages have been throwing "The page build failed for the
branch with the following error" or "The page build failed for the
branch with the following error" emails. So this post.
The case I ran into — after

The page build failed for the `master` branch with the following error:

Unable to build page. Please try again later.

For information on troubleshooting Jekyll see:

  https://docs.github.com/articles/troubleshooting-jekyll-builds

If you have any questions you can submit a request at https://support.github.com/contact?repo_id=180706716&page_build_id=253626373
The page build failed for the `master` branch with the following error:
Unable to build page. Please try again later.
For information on troubleshooting Jekyll see:
If you have any questions you can submit a request at https://support.github.com/contact?repo_id=180706716&page_build_id=253626373
GitHub Pages is trying to compile your site as Jekyll
If it's the first one, you basically wait for GitHub to fix it. At the time of writing (2021/05/16), a lot of people were reporting "GitHub Page build failure":
Unable to build page. Please try again later
Before I checked GitHub Community, I dug into common causes — including GitHub Pages mistakenly compiling your static site as Jekyll. Jekyll is a static site generator written in Ruby — see
To stop this, the workaround is to drop an empty file named
at the root of the deployed site. GitHub Pages sees that file and skips Jekyll entirely.

# Include / Exclude file(s)
## include:/exclude: options only apply to the 'source/' folder
include:
  - .nojekyll
exclude:
ignore:
## include:/exclude: options only apply to the 'source/' folder
Critical step — if you skip the next bit, the
file won't actually deploy. Update your

deploy:
  type: git
  repo: https://github.com/hsiangfeng/hsiangfeng.github.io.git
  branch: master
  ignore_hidden: false
That's the precaution — keeps GitHub Pages from grabbing your site as Jekyll input.
You can also check overall GitHub service health at

Deployed Hexo (or any static site) to GitHub Pages and got "The page build failed for the master/main branch" email? Two common causes — either GitHub Pages is having an outage, or GitHub Pages is trying to compile your site as Jekyll. This post covers the .nojekyll workaround and how to make Hexo include hidden files.
